title: Feature X-ray — tag filtering
question: How does tag filtering actually work in the skills catalog?
tldr: Every render is derived from the URL: parseTagFromSearch reads the single ?tag= parameter, filterByTag narrows visibleSkills() by inclusion, and app.mjs replaces the list DOM. Without JavaScript the full catalog with plain ?tag= chip links is already complete — the script only enhances it.

map.lede: Four small modules, one dependency direction: the DOM depends on pure functions, never the reverse.
map.caption: Solid edges run on every navigation and popstate. The dark node is the only mutable surface in the feature.

steps.title: Identify the requested tag
steps.what: parseTagFromSearch(location.search) reads the single ?tag= parameter via URLSearchParams and returns the tag string or null. Duplicate parameters resolve to the first occurrence; there is deliberately no multi-tag syntax.

steps.title: Narrow the catalog
steps.what: filterByTag(skills, tag) returns the full list for a null tag and filters by tags.includes(tag) otherwise, preserving input order. It is a plain export — node --test imports it directly.

steps.title: Re-derive the DOM
steps.what: renderFromUrl() pipes the two pure functions and calls replaceChildren with either the visible rows or — when a tag is present and the list is empty — the accepted explain-and-suggest empty state.

steps.title: History traversal replays the same path
steps.what: popstate re-invokes renderFromUrl; there is no separate history handler, so back/forward can never diverge from a fresh load of the same URL.

usage.lede: Adding a taggable skill requires no middleware knowledge — tags live next to the skill entry and the chips derive from them.

gotchas.lead: The no-JS page is the baseline, not a fallback.
gotchas.body: index.html ships the complete catalog with working ?tag= links; app.mjs enhances it after load. Testing with JavaScript disabled exercises a real supported path.

gotchas.lead: A missing tag and an unknown tag are different states.
gotchas.body: null (no ?tag=) renders the full catalog; an unknown tag renders the empty state. Collapsing the two was the root cause of incident INC-0721.

gotchas.lead: Order stability is asserted, not assumed.
gotchas.body: filterByTag preserves visibleSkills() order by construction, and test/catalog.test.mjs pins it — reorderings are test failures, not surprises.

faq.q: How do I add a new tag?
faq.a: Add it to any skill's tags array — chips and filters derive from the data; nothing else changes.

faq.q: Can users filter by two tags?
faq.a: No — multi-tag was an explicit Gate A non-goal. The URL schema (single ?tag=) would need a new spec round.

faq.q: Where do I see what renders for an unknown tag?
faq.a: Open any ?tag= value that no skill carries; the accepted empty state (interface-directions, direction B) renders with the real tag set as chips.
